completion_summary: Rebuilt the public documentation around five user journeys and removed maintainer-only paths; updated the current benchmark, mechanism, RL and live-LLM evidence boundaries; added a public-doc fail-closed audit; replaced the old review manuscript with an eleven-page Nature Article-style submission draft, five evidence-bound figures, source tables, a four-level claim ledger and a rendered PDF. Repaired stale partition recipe-dimension and learning-budget tests, backend candidate digests, score-replay/exploit evidence bindings, and an online-resource exploit probe that no longer supplied an explicit provider-call limit. The complete 72-probe exploit matrix passes. Ruff, strict MkDocs build, manuscript/public-doc audits, PDF visual inspection and the complete test suite pass (1099 passed, 14 skipped, 90% coverage). Main was pushed directly and the generated site was pushed to gh-pages without Actions; the live page returned HTTP 200 with the new five-group navigation. Formal benchmark evidence remains incomplete by design (0/10 formal components), so the manuscript is an author-review submission draft rather than a publication-ready claim.
notes: Public documentation must contain only user-facing concepts and released evidence; maintainer claim paths, workstream commands, contributor branches, and tuning seeds stay out of site navigation. Manuscript remains evidence-gated and must not convert development controls into confirmatory claims.
scope: Rebuild the public documentation architecture from current evidence, remove maintainer-only information, update and expand the Nature-style manuscript and source data, render and audit the PDF, repair release-blocking stale dimensions and evidence digests, build the static site, and publish both main and gh-pages without CI
